title: Form 17E — Trial Management Conference Brief
description: Prepared by each party before a trial management conference. Summarizes the issues going to trial, witnesses, evidence, time estimate, and any remaining settlement possibilities. Required under Family Law Rules Rule 17(13).

steps.title: Case Identification
steps.description: Confirm the court and case details. This brief must be filed and served at least 7 days before the trial management conference.

fields.label: Courthouse
options: St. Catharines — Superior Court of Justice
Thunder Bay — Superior Court of Justice

fields.label: Court file number
Applicant's full legal name
fields.placeholder: e.g. Jane Marie Smith

fields.label: Respondent's full legal name
fields.placeholder: e.g. John Robert Smith

fields.label: You are completing this brief as
Date of the trial management conference
fields.helpText: This brief must be served on the other party and filed with the court at least 7 days before this date.

fields.label: Scheduled trial date (if set)
fields.helpText: If a trial date has already been scheduled, enter it here. Leave blank if not yet set.

steps.title: Issues Going to Trial
steps.description: List every issue that has NOT been resolved and must be decided by the judge at trial. Be thorough — issues not listed here may not be raised at trial.

fields.label: Issues remaining for trial (select all that apply)
fields.helpText: Only check issues that are still genuinely in dispute. Settled issues should not be listed.

options.label: Whether spousal support should be paid (entitlement)
Division of a specific asset (pension, business, investment)

fields.label: Describe the other trial issue
fields.placeholder: e.g. Interpretation of a term in the separation agreement

fields.label: Have any issues been resolved since the last conference?

options.label: No — all listed issues remain in dispute

fields.label: Describe what has been resolved
fields.placeholder: e.g. Child support has been agreed at $850/month effective September 1, 2025. Only the issue of parenting time and spousal support remain for trial.

steps.title: Your Position on Each Issue
steps.description: For each issue going to trial, state your position clearly. The judge will read this to understand what you are asking for and why.

fields.label: Your position on decision-making responsibility (custody)
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am asking for sole decision-making responsibility. The other party and I are unable to communicate effectively about the children's medical and educational decisions. The children's school records show I have been their primary caregiver throughout the relationship and since separation.
fields.helpText: State clearly what you are asking for and the key facts that support your position. Keep it to the point.

fields.label: Your position on parenting time
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am asking that the children reside primarily with me on a schedule of: school weeks with me, alternating weekends with the other party (Friday 6 p.m. to Sunday 6 p.m.), and equal sharing of statutory holidays. This mirrors the arrangement that has been in place since separation and is working well for the children.

fields.label: Your position on child support
fields.placeholder: e.g. Based on the other party's annual income of $82,000 (as disclosed in their financial statement), the Child Support Guidelines amount for two children is $1,340/month. I am asking for this amount plus contribution to the children's extracurricular and medical expenses (Section 7).

fields.label: Your position on spousal support
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am asking for spousal support of $1,200/month for a period of 8 years. I left full-time employment in 2018 to care for the children at the other party's request, losing significant career advancement. The Spousal Support Advisory Guidelines range is $900–$1,500/month for 6–12 years based on our incomes.

fields.label: Your position on property division / equalization
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am asking for an equalization payment of $47,000 based on my net family property calculation. The main assets in dispute are the RRSP ($62,000), the car ($18,000), and a business interest the other party has not fully disclosed.

fields.label: Your position on any other trial issues
fields.placeholder: e.g. Regarding costs: the other party has consistently refused to disclose financial information and rejected reasonable settlement offers. I am asking for full recovery costs on a substantial indemnity basis.

steps.title: Witnesses
steps.description: List every witness you plan to call at trial. Include yourself. Failing to list a witness here may prevent you from calling them at trial.

fields.label: Witnesses you plan to call at trial
fields.helpText: List all witnesses including yourself. You do not need to list the other party — they are not your witness.

fields.label: Witness name
fields.placeholder: e.g. Jane Smith (myself) or Dr. Sarah Lee

fields.label: Role or relationship to the case
fields.placeholder: e.g. Applicant / Mother, Treating physician, Teacher at child's school, Accountant

fields.label: What will this witness testify about?
fields.placeholder: e.g. Will testify about the children's daily routine, the parenting arrangements since separation, and the Respondent's involvement (or lack thereof) in school and medical appointments.

fields.label: Estimated time for this witness (examination + cross-examination)
Is this an expert witness?

options.label: Yes — qualified expert (e.g. psychologist, appraiser, accountant)

fields.label: Has the expert's report been served on the other party?

steps.title: Evidence & Documents
steps.description: Identify the key documents and evidence you plan to use at trial.

fields.label: Key documents you plan to use at trial (select all that apply)

options.label: Income tax returns and Notices of Assessment

fields.label: Describe the other documents
fields.placeholder: e.g. Social media posts, travel records, immigration documents

fields.label: Is your portion of the Continuing Record up to date?
fields.helpText: The Continuing Record is the official binder of all documents filed in your case. You are responsible for keeping your section organized and filed with the court.

options.label: Yes — my portion of the Continuing Record is up to date
No — I need to add documents before trial
I'm not sure — I will confirm with the court clerk

fields.label: Are there any documents you still need from the other party or a third party?

options.label: No — I have all the documents I need

fields.label: Describe what documents you still need and from whom
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am still waiting for the other party's business financial records for 2022–2024, which they have not produced despite being ordered to do so. I may need to bring a motion to compel production before trial.

steps.title: Time Estimate for Trial
steps.description: The judge needs to know how long trial will take to allocate court time properly.

fields.label: How many days do you estimate your case will take (your witnesses + your cross-examinations of the other party's witnesses)?
What is your estimate for the total length of trial (both parties combined)?
fields.helpText: This is your estimate for the whole trial — your side and the other party's side combined.

fields.label: Are there any scheduling constraints the court should know about?
fields.placeholder: e.g. The expert witness (Dr. Sarah Lee) is not available in August. One of the parties has pre-booked travel July 10–17. The school-year schedule affects the children and I would prefer a trial date after September.
fields.helpText: List any dates to avoid for any witness or party, or any other scheduling considerations.

fields.label: Does any party or witness need a court interpreter?
What language(s) does the interpreter need to speak?
fields.placeholder: e.g. French, Punjabi, Mandarin

fields.label: Are there any accessibility or accommodation needs for any party or witness?
fields.placeholder: e.g. The Applicant uses a wheelchair and requires an accessible courtroom. One witness has hearing loss and requires a hearing loop system.
fields.helpText: Contact the court's accessibility coordinator as early as possible if accommodations are needed.

steps.title: Settlement Possibilities
steps.description: The trial management conference is a final opportunity to settle. The judge must be satisfied that settlement has been genuinely explored before proceeding to trial.

fields.label: What settlement attempts have been made?

options.label: No settlement attempts have been made

fields.label: Why has the case not settled?
fields.placeholder: e.g. The main obstacle is the other party's refusal to provide full financial disclosure, which makes it impossible to accurately value the property and calculate support. We have exchanged offers to settle on parenting but remain $400/month apart on child support.
fields.helpText: Be specific and factual. Identifying the real barrier to settlement often helps the judge assist at the conference.

fields.label: Are you still open to settling some or all issues before trial?

options.label: Yes — I am open to settling all remaining issues
Yes — I am open to settling some issues but not all
No — I believe trial is necessary for all remaining issues

fields.label: Which issues are you still willing to negotiate on?
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am willing to negotiate the spousal support duration and amount. I am not willing to accept anything less than primary parenting time given the children's established routine with me.

fields.label: Is there a formal settlement offer currently outstanding?
fields.helpText: A formal offer to settle (Rule 18) can affect who pays costs at the end of trial if it is rejected and the other party does not do better at trial.

options.label: Yes — I made an offer that has not been accepted
Yes — the other party made an offer I have not accepted
No — there is no outstanding offer

steps.title: Orders Sought at the Conference
steps.description: At the trial management conference, the judge may make procedural orders. Identify what you are asking for.

fields.label: At the trial management conference, I am asking the judge to

options.label: Confirm or set the trial date
Order the other party to provide outstanding disclosure before trial
Set limits on the number of witnesses each party may call
Set deadlines for serving expert reports
Order witnesses to be excluded from the courtroom until called
Bifurcate (split) the trial — hear some issues first
Make a costs order based on conduct in the proceedings

fields.label: Describe the other order you are seeking at the TMC
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am asking the judge to order that the parties attend a joint pre-trial meeting to attempt resolution on the financial issues with the assistance of a financial neutral.

fields.label: Are there any urgent matters that have arisen recently that the court should know about?
fields.placeholder: e.g. The other party recently informed me they intend to relocate to another province with the children before the trial date. I may need to bring an emergency motion to prevent relocation pending trial.
fields.helpText: Include anything that has changed since the last court attendance that affects how the trial should proceed.

steps.title: Declaration
steps.description: Confirm the accuracy of your trial management conference brief before filing.

fields.label: I confirm this Trial Management Conference Brief

options.label: Is accurate and complete to the best of my knowledge
Has been or will be served on the other party at least 7 days before the conference
Has been or will be filed with the court at least 7 days before the conference

fields.label: Date of completion
Declaration
fields.text: I declare that the information in this Trial Management Conference Brief is true and complete to the best of my knowledge and belief.
fields.checkboxLabel: I confirm this declaration is true and accurate.

pdfMapping.notes: Form 17E — Trial Management Conference Brief. Filed with court and served on all parties at least 7 days before the trial management conference. See ontario.ca/page/family-law-forms for the current version.
